cases.title: Fozard v. North Texas Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Mr. Chandler Fozard complained that Fort Worth Presbyterian Church's policy restricting the integration of special case felons (including sex offenders) into the congregation violated Scripture. The SJC denied the complaint 20-1, finding the complainant had not demonstrated the Session violated Scripture or the Constitution, while criticizing the Session's arguments from BCO Preliminary Principles 1 and 2 as improper.

cases.title: Ganzel v. Central Florida Presbytery
precedent_refs: TE Thomas Troxell vs. The Presbytery of the Southwest
cases.synopsis: TE Neal Ganzel appealed his conviction and six-month suspension by Central Florida Presbytery for abuse of spiritual authority and related charges. The SJC unanimously sustained both specifications of error, finding that CFP violated BCO 32-20's one-year statute of limitations for most charges and committed numerous procedural missteps that rendered the disciplinary process significantly flawed and prejudicial, reversing the entire censure.
